channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippine VP Sara Duterte impeachment heads to plenary vote amid coercion claims
item.description: Duterte-Carpio's camp has accused administration allies of pressuring legislators to support impeachment or face consequences, as the case moves toward a full chamber vote.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇶🇦 Russian and Qatari foreign ministers discuss Strait of Hormuz situation
item.description: Lavrov and his Qatari counterpart discussed developments around the strategic waterway amid ongoing US-Iran tensions and maritime security concerns.

item.title: 🛢️ API reports large crude oil draw of 8.1 million barrels
item.description: The American Petroleum Institute estimated significant inventory declines, with crude down 8.1 million barrels, indicating tightening supply dynamics.

item.title: 🇩🇪 German Chancellor Merz rules out snap elections or minority government
item.description: Friedrich Merz dismissed speculation about early elections, committing to stable governance as his new administration takes shape.

item.title: ⛽ Gas prices expected to cool drastically in five states
item.description: After last week's spike, gas prices in five states are projected to decline significantly, offering relief to consumers amid regional volatility.

item.title: 💰 US Industries Split Over Trump Tariff Probe on Factory Capacity
item.description: Domestic US industries are urging higher tariffs to curb exports from China and other countries with excess factory capacity, while import-dependent and agriculture groups advocate a more cautious approach to new trade restrictions.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Sanctions Network Exploiting Migrants and Supplying Russian Drone Industry
item.description: Britain sanctioned 35 people and entities involved in recruiting vulnerable migrants to fight for Russia against Ukraine and produce drones for the conflict. The measures aim to disrupt Russia's drone production capabilities.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Steps Up Medium-Range Drone Strikes on Russian Forces
item.description: Ukraine is intensifying its medium-range drone strikes targeting Russian military positions and infrastructure, escalating the conflict's technological warfare dimension.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Limits Mobile Internet Before Scaled-Back Victory Day Parade
item.description: Russian authorities are restricting mobile internet access in Moscow ahead of a more modest Victory Day celebration, reflecting security concerns and wartime realities.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Trump and Xi to Discuss Taiwan in Upcoming Talks
item.description: President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping are expected to hold talks next week with Taiwan as a key agenda item amid ongoing tensions in the Indo-Pacific region.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey Ramps Up Steel Dome Air Defense System Production
item.description: Turkey's Aselsan is accelerating production of Steel Dome air defense components, boosting domestic defense manufacturing capabilities.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Denies UAE Attacks, Warns of Crushing Response
item.description: Iran officially denied conducting attacks against the UAE in recent days while warning of a crushing response to any aggression, amid heightened regional tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇱 German FM Backs Israel Presence in South Lebanon
item.description: Germany's foreign minister affirmed Israel's right to maintain positions in southern Lebanon while cautioning about the broader consequences of prolonged conflict.

item.title: 🛡️ NATO Needs Policies for Sharing AI-Generated Intel
item.description: A NATO official emphasized the need for new policies to enable sharing of AI-generated intelligence across alliance members as artificial intelligence transforms military intelligence operations.

item.title: 🇨🇦 Alberta Separatists Submit Signatures for Independence Referendum
item.description: Alberta separatists have submitted petition signatures seeking a referendum on independence from Canada, reflecting growing western alienation in the country.

item.title: ⚖️ Supreme Court Restores Telehealth Access to Abortion Pill Mifepristone
item.description: The US Supreme Court restored telehealth access to the abortion pill mifepristone, rolling back restrictions that had limited remote prescriptions of the medication.

item.title: 🌍 Gulf States Have Mixed Feelings About US After Iran War, Expert Warns
item.description: Regional expert Gregory Gause warned that Gulf states have complicated reactions to US actions in the Iran conflict, with some viewing American intervention positively while others remain skeptical of long-term US commitment.

item.title: 🏛️ US-Bahrain Draft UN Resolution Seeks Sanctions Over Iran Strait of Hormuz Attacks
item.description: The United States and Bahrain are drafting a UN resolution calling for sanctions against Iran over attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, as Washington builds a maritime coalition.

item.title: ⛽ Dutch Gas Prices Edge Lower as US Moves to Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Dutch natural gas prices declined slightly as US efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz eased supply concerns in European energy markets.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russian Court Seizes Billionaire's Stake in Farming Giant for State
item.description: A Russian court ruled that a billionaire's stake in agricultural company Rusagro must be transferred to state ownership, continuing the pattern of state asset seizures.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Two Russian Hypersonic Scientists Get 12 Years for Treason
item.description: Two more Russian scientists working on hypersonic technology received 12-year prison sentences for treason, amid ongoing crackdowns on defense researchers accused of sharing sensitive information.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel signals return to war in Gaza as ceasefire talks stall
item.description: Analysis suggests Israel is preparing to resume military operations in Gaza as ceasefire negotiations have stalled, with humanitarian concerns mounting over the enclave's population.

item.title: 🇹🇭 Thailand cancels 25-year oil pact with Cambodia after border clashes
item.description: Thailand terminated a quarter-century oil exploration agreement with Cambodia following armed clashes at their shared border, escalating diplomatic tensions between the Southeast Asian neighbors.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Boko Haram kills 23 Chadian soldiers in Lake Chad attack
item.description: Boko Haram militants killed 23 Chadian soldiers in an attack near Lake Chad, underscoring the persistent threat from Islamist insurgents in the region despite years of military operations.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine corruption probe shadows top US negotiator
item.description: A corruption investigation in Ukraine is casting shadows over Kyiv's top negotiator with Washington, though allies maintain that trust in the diplomatic relationship remains intact.
